6.note: Yet once more, in a little while, I will shake the heavens and the earth and the sea and the dry land — Hebrews cites Haggai 2:6 as pointing to the definitive eschatological shaking: the removal of created things that are shakeable, so that only the unshakeable kingdom remains; the new creation will be established through a shaking that removes the old

9.note: The latter glory of this house shall be greater than the former — Haggai promises that the second temple will surpass Solomon's in glory; this is fulfilled not in the physical Herodian temple but in Jesus entering the temple (John 2:19-21: destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up; the temple was his body); the presence of Christ in the second temple makes its latter glory exceed its former
